races.name: Volta Ciclista a Catalunya – Stage 2
races.description: The most likely sprint day of the entire Volta — reversing last year's Banyoles–Figueres route through the hilly Girona interior, with 1,900m of climbing concentrated in the first two-thirds before a fast, flat run-in to Banyoles where Mark Cavendish, Petacchi and Zabel have all sprinted to victory in years past; race leader Dorian Godon defends the jersey after his photo-finish win over Evenepoel in Stage 1.

broadcasts.notes: Stage 2 is still on Discovery+ — the switch to HBO Max does not happen until Stage 4 (Thursday 26 March). TNT Sports TV channels carry the broadcast. Discovery+ subscription ~£30.99/month (or new £25.99/month Saver Plan on HBO Max from Thursday).
Volta a Catalunya is an ASO race — exclusive to Peacock in the US. Premium plan $10.99/month or $109.99/year. Not on HBO Max or FloBikes in the US.
Free-to-air on Spanish national public broadcaster.
Eurosport coverage across 50+ countries. Stream via Discovery+ or HBO Max depending on country.
Claro Sports for most of Latin America; Caracol/RCN specifically for Colombia.

data_confidence.notes: Start time 13:10 CET / 12:10 GMT and finish ~17:10 CET confirmed by PCS result page and multiple preview sources. Distance 167.4km confirmed by PCS and official Volta website. GC context: after Stage 1, Dorian Godon (INEOS Grenadiers) leads in the race leader's jersey; Remco Evenepoel (Red Bull-BORA) sits 2nd on bonus seconds, 6 seconds clear of other GC rivals including Vingegaard, Almeida and Pidcock. Stage 2 favourites updated to reflect the sprint-suited finish and post-Stage 1 form. NOTE: Settimana Internazionale Coppi e Bartali 2026 does NOT start today — all Italian official sources (FCI, GS Emilia, oasport, italciclismo, SpazioCiclismo) confirm it runs March 25–29; Cyclingnews's stage page erroneously shows Stage 1 as March 24.

issues: Settimana Internazionale Coppi e Bartali 2026 Stage 1 date conflict: Cyclingnews stages page lists Stage 1 as 2026-03-24, but all Italian primary sources (FCI official race listing, GS Emilia organisers, oasport.it, italciclismo.it, SpazioCiclismo) unanimously confirm the race runs 25–29 March. Stage 1 is excluded from today's file accordingly.
Stage 2 finish time (~17:10 CET) is an estimate based on start time and typical race speed; PCS lists no average speed yet.
UK platform transition note: Stages 1–3 stream on Discovery+; from Stage 4 (Thursday 26 March) streaming moves to HBO Max. TV broadcast on TNT Sports channels is unaffected throughout.
US broadcaster is Peacock (via ASO rights), not HBO Max or FloBikes — this is an important distinction for US cycling fans.
No other UCI 1.1 or above races identified on 24 March 2026 beyond Volta a Catalunya Stage 2.
